member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor
member.summary: Represents a discovered processor with its manifest and file system locations.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor.Manifest
member.summary: The parsed processor manifest

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor.ManifestPath
member.summary: Absolute path to the plugin.json manifest file

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor.ProcessorDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to the processor's directory

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor.ProcessorPath
member.summary: Absolute path to the processor file

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor.EntryPoint
member.summary: Entry point within the processor file (optional). For DLLs: fully-qualified class name — ProcessorExecutor loads this specific class. When null, ProcessorExecutor finds first class implementing IProcessor. For scripts: function name passed to the executor via {entryPoint} placeholder.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiscoveredProcessor.Roles
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of roles assigned to the distributed application group.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.DAGRole
member.summary: Selection of roles for distributed application group (DAG) assignment.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.DAGRole.Producer
member.summary: Processor has no dependencies and can run in the first batch.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.DAGRole.Transformer
member.summary: Transformer has the same tags for requires and provides.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.DAGRole.Consumer
member.summary: Consumer has tags in requires that are not in provides.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.DAGRole.Sink
member.summary: Consumer with 'pipeline-sink' tag in requires, indicating it should run in the last batch and not be assigned to a DAG group.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlan
member.summary: The result of GetExecutionPlan: DAG structure and execution batches for a given set of processor IDs. Valid = false means the pipeline cannot execute; check Errors for details.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlan.Valid
member.summary: True when the pipeline is structurally valid (no unknown IDs, no cycles, all requires satisfied).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlan.Errors
member.summary: Human-readable validation errors. Populated when Valid = false.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlan.Nodes
member.summary: One entry per processor in the plan, including its batch index.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlan.Edges
member.summary: Directed edges: each edge means FromId must complete before ToId can start.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlan.Batches
member.summary: Execution batches in order. Each inner list is a set of processor IDs that run in parallel. Batches execute sequentially; processors within a batch execute concurrently.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlanNode
member.summary: A single node in the execution plan DAG.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlanNode.Id
member.summary: Processor ID.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlanNode.Requires
member.summary: Context keys this processor requires before it can run.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlanNode.Provides
member.summary: Context keys this processor writes after it runs.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlanNode.OptionalRequires
member.summary: Context keys this processor uses if present but does not require.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ExecutionPlanNode.BatchIndex
member.summary: Zero-based index of the batch this processor belongs to.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDefinition
member.summary: Defines a named pipeline loaded from a .json file in a pipelines/ configuration directory. Lookup key is the filename without extension (case-insensitive). The name field is display-only; the filename is the authoritative identifier.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDefinition.Name
member.summary: Human-readable display name for the pipeline. Not used for lookup — filename-without-extension is the lookup key.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDefinition.Description
member.summary: Optional description of what this pipeline does.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDefinition.Processors
member.summary: Ordered list of processor IDs to execute. Equivalent to ProcessingOptions.Pipeline when resolved.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDefinition.Config
member.summary: Base configuration for the pipeline. Merged with caller's ProcessingOptions.Config, with caller values taking precedence.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineKeys
member.summary: Reserved keys for processor requires/provides. Use these in plugin manifests so the engine applies special DAG behavior.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineKeys.Sink
member.summary: Magic requires key: processor runs after every other node in the pipeline. No processor provides it; the engine treats it as satisfied and adds edges from all other nodes to this one. Use for sinks (e.g. JSON logger, markdown reporter, context dump) that must run last.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineKeys.ProcessorErrors
member.summary: Context key where the engine injects processor failures. Never pruned so sinks and callers always see errors.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineKeys.PipelineNotifications
member.summary: Optional context key: bag for pipeline-level notifications (warnings, status). Any processor can add to it; sinks can read it. Never pruned.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingResult
member.summary: Result of processing files through the Modulus pipeline

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingResult.Success
member.summary: Whether processing completed successfully

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingResult.ProcessorResults
member.summary: Results from all processors in the pipeline Each processor adds its output to the Context

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingResult.Context
member.summary: Final context after pipeline execution Contains all processor outputs (findings, metrics, caches, etc.) Applications interpret Context keys based on their domain Example: IArchitecture looks for Context["violations"]

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingResult.Diagnostics
member.summary: Processing diagnostics (errors and warnings)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingResult.Metadata
member.summary: Metadata about the processing execution

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.BatchProcessingResult
member.summary: Batch processing result for multiple files

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.BatchProcessingResult.Success
member.summary: Whether all processing completed successfully.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.BatchProcessingResult.TotalProcessed
member.summary: Total number of items processed.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.BatchProcessingResult.Results
member.summary: Individual processing results.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.BatchProcessingResult.Diagnostics
member.summary: Diagnostics collected during batch processing.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.BatchProcessingResult.Metadata
member.summary: Metadata about the batch processing execution.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingDiagnostic
member.summary: Processing diagnostic (error or warning)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingDiagnostic.Message
member.summary: Diagnostic message describing the issue.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingDiagnostic.Exception
member.summary: Optional exception that caused this diagnostic.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingDiagnostic.Severity
member.summary: Severity level of the diagnostic.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiagnosticSeverity
member.summary: Diagnostic severity level

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiagnosticSeverity.Warning
member.summary: Non-fatal issue that should be investigated.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.DiagnosticSeverity.Error
member.summary: Fatal issue that prevented successful processing.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingMetadata
member.summary: Metadata about a processing execution

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingMetadata.RunId
member.summary: Unique id for this pipeline run (for tracing and correlation).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingMetadata.EngineVersion
member.summary: Modulus engine version

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingMetadata.Timestamp
member.summary: Timestamp when processing started

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessingMetadata.PipelineDiagnostics
member.summary: Pipeline diagnostics (DAG shape and per-processor timing) when processing options have pipeline diagnostics enabled.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDiagnostics
member.summary: Pipeline run diagnostics: DAG structure and per-processor timing. Populated when diagnostics are enabled in processing options.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDiagnostics.Nodes
member.summary: Nodes in the execution DAG (id, requires, provides).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDiagnostics.Edges
member.summary: Edges in the DAG (FromId runs before ToId).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDiagnostics.Batches
member.summary: Execution batches: batch index → list of processor ids run in parallel in that batch.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.PipelineDiagnostics.ProcessorTimings
member.summary: Per-processor execution timing (order matches execution order).

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagNodeInfo
member.summary: A node in the pipeline DAG for diagnostics.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagNodeInfo.Id
member.summary: Processor identifier.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagNodeInfo.Requires
member.summary: Context keys required by this processor.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagNodeInfo.Provides
member.summary: Context keys provided by this processor.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagEdge
member.summary: An edge in the pipeline DAG (FromId must run before ToId).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagEdge.FromId
member.summary: Processor that must run first.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.DagEdge.ToId
member.summary: Processor that depends on FromId.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorTiming
member.summary: Timing for a single processor execution.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorTiming.ProcessorId
member.summary: Identifier of the processor that was timed.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorTiming.BatchIndex
member.summary: Batch index in which the processor executed.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorTiming.ElapsedMilliseconds
member.summary: Wall-clock execution time in milliseconds.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry
member.summary: Represents a single processor entry in a multi-processor manifest. Allows one DLL to export multiple processors with different class names.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.Id
member.summary: Unique processor identifier. Used to reference this processor from pipelines.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.EntryPoint
member.summary: Entry point within the processor file. For DLLs: fully-qualified class name (e.g. "ArchitectureValidators.CircularDependencyValidator") For scripts: function name to invoke (e.g. "Invoke-ContextProvider")

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.Name
member.summary: Human-readable processor name (optional, defaults to Id if not provided)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.Type
member.summary: Processor type (defaults to Processor)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.Requires
member.summary: Data contracts: Keys that must exist in pipeline Context for this processor to execute. Examples: ["parsed-files"], ["dependency-graph"], ["language-list"]

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.Provides
member.summary: Data contracts: Keys this processor will add to pipeline Context. Examples: ["call-graph"], ["violations"], ["metrics"]

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.OptionalRequires
member.summary: Context keys this processor will use if present, but does not require. Pipeline is valid even if no one provides them; the processor runs after any provider (for ordering). Use for optional cache or other best-effort inputs.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.SupportsBatching
member.summary: Indicates whether this processor supports batch processing. Defaults to false (single-file processing).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorEntry.ExtensionData
member.summary: Captures any additional JSON properties not mapped to known fields. Allows consumers to attach custom metadata (e.g. IDE hints, categories) without requiring changes to the Modulus SDK model.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest
member.summary: Plugin manifest loaded from plugin.json file. Supports two formats: 1. Single-processor: Id, Name, Type at top level (backward compatible) 2. Multi-processor: Processors array with multiple entries (one DLL, multiple processors)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Id
member.summary: Unique plugin identifier (single-processor format). Used to reference this plugin from rule files or configuration. Required for single-processor format, null for multi-processor format.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Name
member.summary: Human-readable plugin name (single-processor format). Required for single-processor format, null for multi-processor format.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Type
member.summary: Plugin type (single-processor format). Required for single-processor format, null for multi-processor format.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.EntryPoint
member.summary: Entry point within the processor file (optional, single-processor format). For DLLs: fully-qualified class name (e.g. "ArchitectureValidators.CircularDependencyValidator") For scripts: function name to invoke (e.g. "Invoke-ContextProvider") If not specified for DLLs, ProcessorExecutor will find first class implementing IProcessor.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Requires
member.summary: Data contracts: Keys that must exist in pipeline Context (single-processor format).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Provides
member.summary: Data contracts: Keys this processor will add to pipeline Context (single-processor format).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.OptionalRequires
member.summary: Context keys this processor will use if present, but does not require. Used for optional inputs (e.g. cache): the processor runs after any provider of these keys (for ordering), but the pipeline is still valid if no one provides them. At runtime the processor sees context; if the key is missing it may create it, if present it may transform it. Case-insensitive.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.SupportsBatching
member.summary: Indicates whether this plugin supports batch processing (single-processor format). Defaults to false (single-file processing).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Processors
member.summary: Multiple processor entries (multi-processor format). Allows one DLL to export multiple processors with different class names. Example: One ArchitectureValidators.dll with 12 different validator classes. If specified, Id/Name/Type at top level are ignored.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Version
member.summary: Plugin version following semantic versioning (e.g., "1.0.0")

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.ProcessorPath
member.summary:
Path to the processor file (relative to plugin.json). Supported extensions: - .dll: C# compiled assembly (executed via reflection) - .py: Python script (executed via python process) - .ts: TypeScript script (executed via ts-node) - .ps1: PowerShell script (executed via pwsh process)
This path is resolved relative to the plugin.json directory during discovery.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.Dependencies
member.summary: Dependencies required by this plugin. For DLLs: Assembly names or file paths For scripts: Package names (informational - not validated)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorManifest.ExtensionData
member.summary: Captures any additional JSON properties not mapped to known fields. Allows consumers to attach custom metadata (e.g. IDE hints, categories) without requiring changes to the Modulus SDK model.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorType
member.summary: Types of plugins supported by Modulus. Modulus is domain-agnostic - all processing is done via generic processors.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorType.Parser
member.summary: Parser plugin - parses file content into structured data

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorType.Processor
member.summary: Generic processor plugin - transforms, validates, heals, enriches, filters, strips, etc. Domain-specific semantics are defined by the application layer. These plugins receive JSON via stdin and return JSON via stdout.

member.name: F:Modulus.SDK.Models.ProcessorType.Output
member.summary: Output plugin - delivers results to external systems (console, file, webhook, etc.)

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IMatch
member.summary: A single match from a parsed structure query (e.g. a line or region). Defined in Extensions so parsers like SimpleLineParser can expose line-based matches without the SDK depending on a concrete structure type.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IMatch.StartLine
member.summary: Starting line number of the match.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IMatch.EndLine
member.summary: Ending line number of the match.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IMatch.Text
member.summary: Matched text content.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IMatch.Metadata
member.summary: Additional metadata associated with the match.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure
member.summary: Contract for a parsed structure (e.g. line-based, AST) produced by parsers in Modulus.Storage. Downstream processors that depend on "parsed-structure" in context can cast to this interface when they know the pipeline uses a Storage parser (e.g. SimpleLineParser).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure.StructureType
member.summary: Type of parsed structure (e.g. "line-based", "ast").

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure.FilePath
member.summary: Path of the file that was parsed.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure.Format
member.summary: Format identifier for the parsed content.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure.OriginalContent
member.summary: Original file content before parsing.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure.RawData
member.summary: Raw parsed data (implementation-specific).

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Parsers.IParsedStructure.Query(System.String)
member.summary: Queries the parsed structure for matches against the given pattern.
member.returns: Matching regions in the parsed structure.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext
member.summary: Describes where and how the validation was invoked. Allows plugins to adapt behavior based on execution context.
member.example: Examples of context-aware behavior: - Slack plugin: Skip in IDE (too noisy), post in CI/CD (team notification) - SARIF plugin: Different output paths for IDE (.vscode/) vs CI/CD (build/) - Console plugin: Verbose in interactive CLI, terse in CI/CD

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext.Source
member.summary: Source of invocation. Common values: "CLI", "VSCode", "VisualStudio", "GitHubActions", "Jenkins", "GitLabCI", "API"

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext.User
member.summary: User who triggered validation (from IDE or Git context). Null if not available (e.g., scheduled cron job, anonymous API call).

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext.IsInteractive
member.summary: Interactive mode: User is waiting for results (true) vs background execution (false). Affects output verbosity and notification strategies. Examples: - CLI with user waiting: true - IDE file save: true - CI/CD pipeline: false - Scheduled batch scan: false

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext.WorkingDirectory
member.summary: Working directory where validation was invoked

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext.CommandLine
member.summary: Command-line arguments (for CLI invocations)

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.InvocationContext.Properties
T:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.IProcessor
member.summary: Generic processor interface - can analyze, transform, cache, output, etc. Replaces Iprocessor, Itransformer, IOutputPlugin with a unified interface. Everything is a processor: parsers, processors, transformers, outputs, cache builders.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.IProcessor.ProcessAsync(Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput)
member.summary: Process input and return result

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput
member.summary: Input data passed to processors

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput.Context
member.summary: Shared context flowing through the pipeline Processors can read from and write to Context. Common keys: "dependency-graph", "findings", "metrics", etc.

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput.Config
member.summary: Processor configuration Convention: processors read config keys prefixed with their ID Example: "my-processor.max-depth" = "5"

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput.Manifest
member.summary: Plugin manifest (for self-awareness)

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorResult
member.summary: Result of processor execution

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorResult.Success
member.summary: Whether processing succeeded

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorResult.Context
member.summary: Shared context with processor outputs Processors write their outputs here using whatever keys they want. Examples: - Context["findings"] = list of findings - Context["dependency-graph"] = graph object - Context["report"] = report data - Context["metrics"] = stats

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorResult.Error
member.summary: Error message if processing failed

member.name: P:Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorResult.Warnings
member.summary: Non-fatal warnings

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Processors.ProcessorBase
member.summary: Optional base class for processors that provides common patterns. Processors can implement IProcessor directly if they prefer. This base class handles: - Automatic requirement validation from manifest - Consistent error handling - Try/catch wrapper - Context copying

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Processors.ProcessorBase.ValidateRequirements(Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput)
member.summary: Validate requirements. Default: validate manifest requires context keys. Override for custom validation.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Processors.ProcessorBase.ExecuteAsync(Modulus.SDK.Plugins.ProcessorInput)
member.summary: Execute processor logic. Return modified context. Implemented by derived class.
member.param: Processor input with file content, context, config, etc.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Storage.IDataStore
member.summary: Generic abstraction for data storage backends (file, S3, Redis, memory, etc.) Uses byte[] for maximum flexibility - supports text, binary, and compressed formats. Implementations can be composed using decorator pattern for compression, encryption, etc.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Storage.IDataStore.LoadAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Load data as byte array. Could be JSON text, gzipped JSON, protobuf, or any binary format.
member.returns: Data as object, or null if not found

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Storage.IDataStore.SaveAsync(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Save data as byte array.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Storage.IDataStore.ExistsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Check if data exists for the given key.
member.returns: True if data exists, false otherwise

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Storage.IDataStore.DeleteAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Delete data for the given key.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Storage.NullDataStore
member.summary: No-op data store that does nothing. Used as default when no storage is needed. Safe - performs no file system operations.

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper
member.summary: Generic utilities for working with processor context dictionaries. Provides type-safe access to context keys with clear error messages. Domain-agnostic - works with any types.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.GetRequired``1(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String)
member.summary: Gets a required value from context, throws if missing or wrong type.
member.exception: Thrown if key is missing or has wrong type

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.GetOrDefault``1(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String,``0)
member.summary: Gets a value from context, returns default if missing.
member.param: Value to return if key is missing or wrong type
member.returns: Typed value from context or default

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.GetOrNull``1(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String)
member.summary: Gets a value from context, returns null if missing or wrong type.
member.typeparam: Expected type of the value (must be class)
member.returns: Typed value from context or null

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.Set``1(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String,``0)
member.summary: Sets a value in context (create or replace).

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.AppendToList``1(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Appends items to a list in context (creates list if doesn't exist).
member.typeparam: Type of items in the list

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.AppendToList``1(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String,``0)
member.summary: Appends a single item to a list in context (creates list if doesn't exist).
member.typeparam: Type of item in the list

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.ValidateRequired(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.String[])
member.summary: Validates that all required keys exist in context.
member.exception: Thrown if any required keys are missing

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.ContextHelper.ValidateRequiredTypes(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.Type}[])
member.summary: Validates that required keys exist and have correct types.
member.param: Key-type pairs that must be satisfied

member.name: T:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.HierarchyHelper
member.summary: Utilities for working with Modulus hierarchy discovery. Provides file-to-scope resolution so any processor can determine which config anchor applies to a given file without reimplementing walk-up logic.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.HierarchyHelper.GetScope(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Resolves the config scope (anchor path) for a file by walking up the directory chain and returning the first ancestor that is a known config root.
member.remarks: Use this in processors to map a file path to the anchor key in your config dictionary, e.g. InitConfig.ConfigRoots. All processors use the same walk so scope resolution is consistent across the pipeline.
member.param: File or directory path to resolve scope for.
Known config root (anchor) paths. Comparison is ordinal-ignore-case.
Returned when no anchor is found in the chain. Defaults to null.
member.returns: The anchor path that is the closest ancestor of , or if none found.

member.name: M:Modulus.SDK.Utilities.HierarchyHelper.GetAnchors(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{System.String})
member.summary: Returns all ancestor anchor paths for a file, walking up the directory chain. Unlike which returns only the closest, this returns all matches.
member.param: File or directory path to resolve anchors for.
member.returns: List of matching anchor paths from closest to farthest, or null if none found.
